topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

My phone buzzed with a notification, and my stomach immediately clenched into a tight knot. A profile picture popped up: a grainy, slightly distorted photo of a grinning face I hadn't seen in fifteen years—Mark. Mark, the architect of my awkward middle school years. A friend request. My breath hitched. I stared at the screen, my thumb hovering uselessly over the 'decline' button. The silence of my room felt suddenly loud, amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. What on earth was he thinking? I quickly minimized the message, shoving the phone face down on the desk, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

The chime of the notification pulled me out of a deep concentration on my laptop. It was a request from Mark. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, warming my cheeks until they felt tight and prickly. I glanced over my shoulder, half-expecting someone to be watching, though I was utterly alone. His name flashed on the screen, and a wave of unwelcome recollection washed over me—the sneers, the deliberate exclusion. I felt my shoulders tense up, pulling inward as if trying to shrink away from the digital intrusion. I let the request sit there, a glowing, unwelcome artifact on my screen, before finally tapping to dismiss it with a jerky motion.

[story 3]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug when my phone lit up. It was Mark. A friend request. A sudden, sharp feeling of heat flooded my face, making my vision swim for a second. I picked up the device with a hand that trembled slightly. Seeing his name made my throat constrict. I could feel the blood rushing to my ears, a distinctly unpleasant physical reaction. Why now? I quickly navigated to the settings, my fingers moving too fast, trying to erase the digital footprint of this unexpected contact. The silence in the apartment seemed to press in, heavy and suffocating.
